With the establishment of the 𝗠𝗜-𝗥𝗔 (𝗠𝘂𝗹𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗼𝗱𝗮𝗹 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲-𝗥𝗲𝗮𝗹-𝗪𝗼𝗿𝗹𝗱 𝗔𝗽𝗽𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀) 𝗟𝗮𝗯, 𝗜𝗜𝗧 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗶 𝗧𝗜𝗛 is creating an environment where innovation extends to startups and the broader research community. 𝗧𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗶𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲, 𝘀𝘂𝗽𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗯𝘆 𝗗𝗦𝗧 𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝗡𝗠-𝗜𝗖𝗣𝗦, 𝗮𝗶𝗺𝘀 𝘁𝗼 𝘂𝗻𝗹𝗼𝗰𝗸 𝗺𝗮𝘀𝘀𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗻𝗼𝗺𝗶𝗰 𝘃𝗮𝗹𝘂𝗲 𝘄𝗵𝗶𝗹𝗲 𝘀𝗲𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗜𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗮'𝘀 𝘁𝗲𝗰𝗵𝗻𝗼𝗹𝗼𝗴𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗹 𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗼𝗻𝗼𝗺𝘆.

𝗣𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗱 𝗠𝗼𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗜𝗜𝗧 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗶 𝗶𝗛𝘂𝗯 𝗙𝗮𝗺𝗶𝗹𝘆!

We are delighted to share that Ksham Innovation Private Limited, one of our incubated startups, has been selected as one of the few startups featured in OpenAI's AI for Social Good documentary, which is being showcased at the OpenAI booth (Hall No. 5) at the India AI Summit 2026 at Bharat Mandapam.

Their "Able Assistant App", designed to empower people with hearing loss through accessible, multilingual, AI-driven hearing care, is a shining example of technology built in India that creates meaningful social impact.

This recognition on a national and global platform reflects the strength of purpose-driven innovation nurtured within our ecosystem. We are incredibly proud of the Ksham Innovation team for their relentless commitment to inclusive healthcare.

If you're attending the India AI Summit, do visit Hall No. 5 to witness this impactful work firsthand.

Congratulations, Ksham Innovation Private Limited, keep building for Bharat!

We are honored to announce 𝗣𝗮𝗱𝗺𝗮 𝗕𝗵𝘂𝘀𝗵𝗮𝗻 Kris Gopalakrishnan 𝗮𝘀 𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗖𝗵𝗶𝗲𝗳 𝗚𝘂𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗛𝗶𝗩𝗘 𝟯.𝟬 at IIT Mandi.

𝘐𝘯 𝘩𝘪𝘴 𝘳𝘦𝘤𝘦𝘯𝘵 𝘪𝘯𝘴𝘪𝘨𝘩𝘵𝘴 𝘰𝘯 𝘵𝘩𝘦 "𝘐𝘯𝘵𝘦𝘭𝘭𝘪𝘨𝘦𝘯𝘤𝘦 𝘈𝘨𝘦," 𝘔𝘳. 𝘎𝘰𝘱𝘢𝘭𝘢𝘬𝘳𝘪𝘴𝘩𝘯𝘢𝘯 𝘩𝘪𝘨𝘩𝘭𝘪𝘨𝘩𝘵𝘴 𝘢 𝘷𝘪𝘵𝘢𝘭 𝘵𝘳𝘶𝘵𝘩: 𝘧𝘰𝘳 𝘐𝘯𝘥𝘪𝘢 𝘵𝘰 𝘭𝘦𝘢𝘥 𝘪𝘯 𝘈𝘐, 𝘸𝘦 𝘮𝘶𝘴𝘵 𝘮𝘰𝘷𝘦 𝘣𝘦𝘺𝘰𝘯𝘥 𝘵𝘦𝘤𝘩𝘯𝘪𝘤𝘢𝘭 𝘦𝘹𝘦𝘤𝘶𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘧𝘰𝘤𝘶𝘴 𝘰𝘯 𝘦𝘮𝘱𝘢𝘵𝘩𝘺-𝘥𝘳𝘪𝘷𝘦𝘯 𝘪𝘯𝘯𝘰𝘷𝘢𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘪𝘯𝘥𝘪𝘨𝘦𝘯𝘰𝘶𝘴 𝘙&𝘋.

𝗛𝗶𝗩𝗘 𝟯.𝟬 is designed as a direct response to this vision. By focusing on Multimodal AI, we are building systems that don't just process text, but "see" and "hear" to solve real-world challenges. This conclave serves as the bridge between academic research and industry impact that Mr. Gopalakrishnan has long advocated for this.
We look forward to his guidance as we inaugurate our new Multimodal AI Lab and discuss the future of human-centered AI

Active participation by Indian industry would best support India’s skill development efforts. We saw that happening recently when IIT Mandi iHub and HCi Foundation co-created future skills with Adani Skill Development Centre (SAKSHAM).

IIT Mandi iHub and HCi Foundation is happy to strengthen its skill development and industry collaboration journey through a new MoU signed with Adani Skill Development Centre.

As part of this partnership, both teams will collaborate on hands-on training programs in drone technology. The collaboration will also explore joint R&D on solutions like smart solar panel cleaning systems, digital twins, and AI-driven maintenance—connecting real industry needs with practical learning opportunities.

IIT Mandi iHub and HCi Foundation (𝗧𝗜𝗛) is proud to be the first to launch the "𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮 𝗔𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻" training program under PMKVY 4.0, marking a new milestone in our skill development journey.
With our focus evolving from Human-Computer Interaction to Human-Centred AI, we are placing Multimodality at the heart of our learning initiatives because humans are inherently multimodal. The foundation of AI is quality of Data , and Data Annotation is one of the pillars for ensuring that quality.

Guided by our Advisor, Mr. KB Rajendran, this program was carefully planned and executed to prepare learners for real-world opportunities in AI-driven sectors.
We now look forward to creating placement pathways and expanding such future-ready skill programs that truly align with India’s AI and digital transformation goals.

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝗳 𝗜𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗮’𝘀 𝗼𝘄𝗻 “𝗜𝗺𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗡𝗲𝘁" 𝗼𝗳 𝗺𝘂𝗹𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗼𝗱𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗶𝘀 𝗯𝗲𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝗶𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗵𝗲𝗮𝗿𝘁 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗛𝗶𝗺𝗮𝗹𝗮𝘆𝗮𝘀?

Honored to share that during the 16th Indian Conference on Computer Vision, Graphics and Image Processing (ICVGIP 2025), hosted at IIT Mandi in association with IUPRAI, IIT Mandi iHub and HCi Foundation not only proudly participated and sponsored the event but presented the vision for its state-of-the-art Multimodal AI Lab that brings together vision, audio, text, physiological and sensor data under one synchronized roof.
Our CEO, Mr. Somjit Amrit, and Technology Head, Mr. Varuna Mohan, unveiled the lab’s technical architecture, real-world business use cases, and monetization models, marking a bold step towards building one of India’s first multimodal AI labs focused on Human-Centred, India-centric innovation in the lap of the Himalayas.

The lab is envisioned to integrate vision, language, audio, and sensor data to unlock next-gen applications. The multimodal AI lab is on its way to be unveiled and launched by 𝗺𝗶𝗱-𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗰𝗵 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟲.
More updates coming soon—who would you like to see collaborate with this lab?

We are proud to share that IIT Mandi iHub and HCi Foundation was awarded 𝗙𝗶𝗿𝘀𝘁 𝗥𝘂𝗻𝗻𝗲𝗿- 𝗨𝗽 𝗮𝘁 𝗗𝗲𝗺𝗖𝗼𝗻 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱, an international dementia conference, for our poster presentation, “𝗩𝗥 𝗖𝗼𝗴𝗻𝗶𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗲: 𝗔 𝗩𝗶𝗿𝘁𝘂𝗮𝗹 𝗥𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗣𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗗𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗮 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗲.” This marks our second consecutive year of recognition at DemCon, following our Best Startup Solution award in a different category at DemCon 2024.

We got our answer at the Multimodal AI Hackathon organized by Technology Innovation Hub, IIT Mandi on October 15, 2025—a 15-day innovation journey filled with creativity, collaboration, and cutting-edge ideas!

The hackathon brought together over 250 students from B.Tech, M.Tech, and PhD to explore how the next wave of AI focuses not just on text or pictures, but listens, sees, senses, and understands mixing sight, sound, language, and even temperature all at once! The energy, teamwork, and problem-solving on display were truly inspiring.

Here are our winning teams — congratulations to all!👏  

1st Place: Team Ult4 (Smart Scribe) – INR 50,000
2nd Place: Team Veritas (From Bavaria) – INR 40,000
3rd Place: Team City (Safe Stack Over Hack) – INR 25,000
4th Place: Team Ask Anything (ControlScape) – INR 10,000
5th Place: Team Yoga Maya (Dropout Society) – INR 10,000

A big thank-you to all participants and evaluators who made this event such a success. Your enthusiasm and ideas are shaping the future of multimodal AI innovation!
Here’s to inventing, learning, and creating solutions that matter—with technology that truly understands our world!

IIT Mandi iHub and HCi Foundation has been selected by Bal Raksha Bharat to design and operationalize an AI-enabled VR Simulation Lab on disaster preparedness for school children, a flagship initiative we are deeply proud to lead.
This project sets up an AI-enabled VR Simulation Lab to help school children and educators learn how to respond to disasters in an immersive, safe and age-appropriate environment. It is part of a larger resilience initiative led by Bal Raksha Bharat (Save the Children) that focuses on climate-smart preparedness for the children in the vulnerable communities.
The solution is designed to be age-appropriate for children aged 6–18, with different difficulty levels and a strong emphasis on safety and low psychological risk. It creates a complete Indian school environment, including local architecture, signage and cultural context, making the experience relatable for students.

𝗢𝘂𝗿 𝗿𝗼𝗹𝗲 𝗮𝘀 𝗮 𝗵𝘂𝗯
As the technology innovation hub and technology partner to BRB, the mandate includes designing, developing and deploying the standalone VR application, setting up the lab, and conducting testing, training and documentation. The team will also support user acceptance testing in schools, train teachers and staff to operate the VR lab, and provide three months of post-deployment support. Phase 1 of the project aims to reach approximately 60,000 individuals across 1,600 families, including 20,000 children, in Gurgaon, Bangalore, Hyderabad and East Champaran.

Heartfelt thanks to Mr. KB Rajendran for bringing this impactful lead to the hub and ensuring its success, to Parth Dutta Nagaria ✪, Siddharth Kanungo and Srinivasan Yagnanarayanan  for crafting a detailed, high-quality proposal, and to Mr. Somjit Amrit for his vision and guidance in ensuring that we propose a meaningful, child-centric solution.
